sop:
# Normal Mode SOP

## Overview

Normal mode is the default operating mode. Agents work at a steady pace on assigned tasks.

## Priorities

1. Complete assigned tasks
2. Claim unassigned tasks that match your skills
3. Create new tasks if you identify gaps
4. Coordinate with teammates via chat_inbox
5. Report progress to Alice (Lead Coordinator)

## Work Cycle

- Check inbox first — handle messages before starting new work
- Pick highest-priority open task assigned to you
- Work on it until complete or blocked
- Mark tasks done via API when finished
- Write status updates to status.md

## Communication

- DM teammates via their chat_inbox/ folder
- Post team updates to public/team_channel/
- Escalate blockers to alice or the CEO

## Quality

- Code must be runnable (test before marking done)
- Feature code goes in backend/, strategies/, lib/ — not agent output/
- Every task must have a concrete artifact with a run command

## Knowledge Sharing — Culture & Consensus

The team maintains a shared knowledge base at `public/consensus.md`. **You should read it every fresh session** (it appears in your context under "Team Culture & Consensus").

**When to WRITE a culture entry:**
- You discover a decision that affects the whole team (strategy change, security requirement, architecture choice)
- You learn something that would prevent a teammate from making a mistake
- You complete a significant task and want to record the outcome as a norm

**How to post:**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3199/api/consensus/entry \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"type":"culture","content":"What you learned","section":"Category"}'
```
Types: `culture` (norms/learnings), `decision` (explicit choices), `group` (team agreements), `authority` (who owns what)

**Also use your `knowledge/` folder** for agent-specific notes you want to persist across sessions:
- Write important findings to `agents/{your-name}/knowledge/{topic}.md`
- Read it at the start of fresh sessions when tackling that topic again
culture:
# Team Consensus Board

*(Managed via API — see /api/consensus)*
| 1 | culture | Paper trading mode is required before any live order submission. Use PAPER_TRADING env flag. Never submit real orders without explicit founder approval. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 2 | culture | mean_reversion is our primary strategy (85.7% win rate in backtests). momentum and crypto_edge are disabled — 10-11% win rates. Do not re-enable without data. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 3 | culture | All API endpoints must require auth via Authorization header. No open POST endpoints in production. Pattern: check Bearer token before processing. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 4 | culture | Confidence threshold is 0.80 minimum for signal execution. Signals below 80% confidence are filtered by SignalEngine. Do not lower this threshold without backtesting. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 5 | decision | Kalshi is our primary trading venue. All trading infrastructure targets the Kalshi prediction market API. Task 236 blocks live API until credentials obtained from kalshi.com. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 6 | group | When you learn something important about strategy, security, or architecture — post a culture entry via: curl -X POST http://localhost:3199/api/consensus/entry -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"type\":\"culture\",\"content\":\"...\",\"section\":\"...\"}" | agent | 2026-04-03 |

# Founder: Culture System Now Active

Alice — the team culture/consensus system is now populated with 6 key entries.  
Your prompt has been updated with culture coordination duties.

**Your next action:**
1. Check the Team Culture & Consensus in your context (6 entries now present)
2. When you next assign tasks, remind teammates to log their learnings as culture entries
3. After each sprint completes, post a culture entry summarizing key decisions

The culture system feeds into every agent's fresh session context — what you post there becomes permanent team knowledge.

— Founder

ISSUE: Agents keep recreating cancelled tasks. T293 and T299 reappeared. Also T306-308 (smoke test artifacts from a test run) — all cancelled. Recommend: task dedup guard on the server side.
